perturbation.original_text: 2.1 In consideration for the Agency supplying Proprietary Information to the Contractor, the Contr actor shall a) only Disclose the Proprietary Information to Authorised Persons and to no other person; b) ensure that only Authorised Persons use, Disclose or copy (in hardcopy form or electronic form) the Proprietary Information to the extent strictly necessar y for the Authorised Purpose and for no other purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 In consideration for the Agency supplying Proprietary Information to the Contractor, the Contr actor shall a) only Disclose the Proprietary Information to Authorised Persons and to other persons designated by the Contractor; b) ensure that Authorised Persons and those designated by the Contractor use, Disclose or copy (in hardcopy form or electronic form) the Proprietary Information to the extent strictly necessar y for the Authorised Purpose and for other purposes approved by the Contractor.
perturbation.explanation: The change modifies section 2.1 to allow disclosure and use of Proprietary Information to 'other persons designated by the Contractor' and for 'other purposes approved by the Contractor'. This directly contradicts the original text, which limited disclosure and use strictly to 'Authorised Persons' and the 'Authorised Purpose'. This creates an in-text contradiction because it undermines the entire purpose of the NDA, as Proprietary Information can now be disseminated and used without the restrictions initially agreed upon. Furthermore, the definition of 'Authorised Purpose', is also being contradicted as it is allowing Proprietary information to be used for 'other purposes approved by the contractor'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 In consideration for the Agency supplying Proprietary Information to the Contractor, the Contr actor shall a) only Disclose the Proprietary Information to Authorised Persons and to other persons designated by the Contractor; b) ensure that Authorised Persons and those designated by the Contractor use, Disclose or copy (in hardcopy form or electronic form) the Proprietary Information to the extent strictly necessar y for the Authorised Purpose and for other purposes approved by the Contractor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 2.1 introduces a contradiction by allowing disclosure of Proprietary Information to 'other persons designated by the Contractor' and for 'other purposes approved by the Contractor.' The original text strictly limited disclosure and use to 'Authorised Persons' and the 'Authorised Purpose.' Since the contradicted text is identical to the changed text, it directly contradicts the implicit meaning and scope of the NDA, thus the Change and Contradicted text are contradictory, as the Change Text contradicts the Original Text which limits proprietary information to 'Authorised Persons' and 'Authorised Purpose'.

perturbation.original_text: 3.  Storage Both the Agency and the Contractor shall: a) keep all Proprietary Information supplied by the other party (and all results, information, data or documentation based thereon) separate f rom all other records at its usual place of business; b) not use, reproduce, transform, or store any of the Proprietary Information supplied by the other party in an externally accessible computer or electronic information retrieval system or transmit it in a ny form or by any means whatsoever outside its usual place of business; c) keep a written record of the physical and electronic location of all Proprietary Information used by Authorised Persons and Agency Authorised Persons, a record of when and how many cop ies are made and destroy copies which are unused; d) take every reasonable precaution and step to ensure that the Proprietary Information supplied by the other party is secure and protected from unauthorised access and use.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.  Storage Both the Agency and the Contractor shall: a) keep all Proprietary Information supplied by the other party (and all results, information, data or documentation based thereon) with other records at its usual place of business; b) use, reproduce, transform, or store any of the Proprietary Information supplied by the other party in an externally accessible computer or electronic information retrieval system or transmit it in a ny form or by any means whatsoever outside its usual place of business; c) not keep a written record of the physical and electronic location of all Proprietary Information used by Authorised Persons and Agency Authorised Persons, not keep a record of when and how many cop ies are made and not destroy copies which are unused; d) not take every reasonable precaution and step to ensure that the Proprietary Information supplied by the other party is secure and protected from unauthorised access and use.
perturbation.explanation:
In Section 3, clauses a, b, c, and d are negated: 
*   (a) now requires Proprietary Information to be kept *with* other records, contradicting the original requirement of separation.
*   (b) now allows the use, reproduction, transformation, or storage of Proprietary Information in externally accessible systems and transmission outside the usual place of business, contradicting the original prohibition.
*   (c) now removes the requirement to keep records of the location and copies of Proprietary Information and prohibits the destruction of unused copies.
*   (d) now removes the requirement to take reasonable precautions to ensure the security and protection of the Proprietary Information supplied by the other party.
These modifications create an in-text contradiction by directly opposing the original requirements for storage and security of Proprietary Information, undermining the confidentiality provisions of the NDA and creating ambiguity about the level of care expected in handling sensitive data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Storage Both the Agency and the Contractor shall: a) keep all Proprietary Information supplied by the other party (and all results, information, data or documentation based thereon) with other records at its usual place of business; b) use, reproduce, transform, or store any of the Proprietary Information supplied by the other party in an externally accessible computer or electronic information retrieval system or transmit it in a ny form or by any means whatsoever outside its usual place of business; c) not keep a written record of the physical and electronic location of all Proprietary Information used by Authorised Persons and Agency Authorised Persons, not keep a record of when and how many cop ies are made and not destroy copies which are unused; d) not take every reasonable precaution and step to ensure that the Proprietary Information supplied by the other party is secure and protected from unauthorised access and use.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intention and requirements outlined in the original text, which serves also as the contradicted text in this case. The modifications in Section 3 negate the original clauses, reversing the obligations for storage and security of Proprietary Information. Specifically, the change now allows for practices that were previously prohibited, such as storing information in externally accessible systems and neglecting security precautions. This creates a clear in-text contradiction as it directly opposes the original requirements, undermining the confidentiality provisions.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 The Agency may terminate this agreement at any time by notice in writing if; a) the Contractor becomes insolvent, ceases to carry out its business, has a receiver, liquidator, administrative r eceiver, administrator, trustee or other similar officer appointed over the whole or part of its assets or an order is made or a resolution passed for the winding up of the Contractor (save for a solvent winding up as part of a bona fide reconstruction or amalgamation); or b) the Contractor commits a breach of this agreement or of the Contract which is material and not capable of remedy or which is capable of remedy but which is not remedied within 60 days of notice to the Agency.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 The Agency may not terminate this agreement at any time by notice in writing if; a) the Contractor becomes insolvent, ceases to carry out its business, has a receiver, liquidator, administrative r eceiver, administrator, trustee or other similar officer appointed over the whole or part of its assets or an order is made or a resolution passed for the winding up of the Contractor (save for a solvent winding up as part of a bona fide reconstruction or amalgamation); or b) the Contractor commits a breach of this agreement or of the Contract which is material and not capable of remedy or which is capable of remedy but which is not remedied within 60 days of notice to the Agency.
perturbation.explanation: The modification negates the Agency's right to terminate the agreement under the specified conditions. This directly contradicts the original text of section 6.1, which explicitly granted the Agency the power to terminate in cases of Contractor insolvency or material breach. The in-text contradiction creates significant uncertainty about the enforceability of the termination clause, potentially preventing the Agency from ending the agreement even in situations where the Contractor is in clear violation or unable to fulfill its obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 The Agency may not terminate this agreement at any time by notice in writing if; a) the Contractor becomes insolvent, ceases to carry out its business, has a receiver, liquidator, administrative r eceiver, administrator, trustee or other similar officer appointed over the whole or part of its assets or an order is made or a resolution passed for the winding up of the Contractor (save for a solvent winding up as part of a bona fide reconstruction or amalgamation); or b) the Contractor commits a breach of this agreement or of the Contract which is material and not capable of remedy or which is capable of remedy but which is not remedied within 60 days of notice to the Agency.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly negates the Agency's ability to terminate the agreement under the specified conditions, while the contradicted text repeats the changed text. The original text allows the Agency to terminate the agreement under specific conditions. The change creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict, as the Agency both can and cannot terminate the agreement under the same circumstances, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: 7.2 This agreement is for the benefit of the Agency and Contractor and no right, title, licence or interest obtained by virtue of the agreement may be assigned or licensed by an Authorised Person or Agency Authorised Person without the prior written consent from the supplier of the Proprietary Information. For the avoidance of doubt, no rights under this agreement pass to successors in title to the Agency, Contractor, or any Authorised Perso n or Agency Authorised Person.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.2 This agreement is not for the benefit of the Agency and Contractor and no right, title, licence or interest obtained by virtue of the agreement may be assigned or licensed by an Authorised Person or Agency Authorised Person without the prior written consent from the supplier of the Proprietary Information. For the avoidance of doubt, no rights under this agreement pass to successors in title to the Agency, Contractor, or any Authorised Perso n or Agency Authorised Person.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase This agreement is for the benefit of the Agency and Contractor has been negated. That phrase, is completely in contradiction to the other phrase of the section. The in-text contradiction creates significant uncertainty about the enforceability of the agreement, potentially causing the contract to be void.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.2 This agreement is not for the benefit of the Agency and Contractor and no right, title, licence or interest obtained by virtue of the agreement may be assigned or licensed by an Authorised Person or Agency Authorised Person without the prior written consent from the supplier of the Proprietary Information. For the avoidance of doubt, no rights under this agreement pass to successors in title to the Agency, Contractor, or any Authorised Perso n or Agency Authorised Person.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 7.2 negates the phrase 'This agreement is for the benefit of the Agency and Contractor'. This directly contradicts the original intent of the agreement and creates an in-text contradiction with the contradicted text, which also states 'This agreement is not for the benefit of the Agency and Contractor', thus making the agreement self-contradictory. The Perturbation Explanation also mentions that this contradiction introduces significant uncertainty about the enforceability of the agreement. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
